heading: Agriculture Advanced Research and Development Authority pilot
label: 3319k Agriculture Advanced Research and Development Authority pilot

children.label: A
children.text: targeted acceleration of novel, early stage innovative agricultural research with promising technology applications and products; or

children.label: i
children.text: prototype testing, preclinical development, or field experimental use;

children.label: ii
children.text: assessing and assisting with product approval, clearance, or need for a license under an applicable law, as determined by the Director; or

children.label: iii
children.text: manufacturing and commercialization of a product.

children.heading: Agricultural technology
children.label: 2 Agricultural technology
children.text: The term “agricultural technology” means machinery and other equipment engineered for an applicable and novel use in agriculture, natural resources, and food relating to the research and development of qualified products and projects.

children.heading: Director
children.label: 3 Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the Agriculture Advanced Research and Development Authority established under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Other transaction
children.label: 4 Other transaction
children.text: The term “other transaction” means a transaction other than a procurement contract, grant, or cooperative agreement, including a transaction described in subsection (b)(6)(A).

children.label: A
children.text: an individual;

children.label: B
children.text: a partnership;

children.label: C
children.text: a corporation;

children.label: D
children.text: an association;

children.label: E
children.text: an entity;

children.label: F
children.text: a public or private corporation;

children.label: G
children.text: a Federal, State, or local government agency or department; and

children.label: H
children.text: an institution of higher education, including a land-grant college or university and a non-land-grant college of agriculture.

children.label: A
children.text: engineering, mechanization, or technology improvements that will address challenges relating to growing, harvesting, handling, processing, storing, packing, and distribution of agricultural products;

children.label: i
children.text: replacement or resistant plant cultivars or varieties;

children.label: ii
children.text: other enhanced management strategies, including novel chemical, biological, or cultural approaches; or

children.label: iii
children.text: diagnostic or surveillance technology; and

children.label: i
children.text: animal vaccine or therapeutic products (including anti-infective products); or

children.label: ii
children.text: diagnostic or surveillance technology.

children.heading: Research tool
children.label: 7 Research tool
children.text: The term “research tool” means a device, technology, procedure, biological material, reagent, computer system, computer software, or analytical technique that is developed to assist in the discovery, development, or manufacture of a qualified product or project.

children.heading: Agriculture Advanced Research and Development Authority
children.label: b Agriculture Advanced Research and Development Authority

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established within the Department of Agriculture a pilot program that shall be known as the Agriculture Advanced Research and Development Authority (referred to in this section as the “AGARDA”) to carry out advanced research and development.

children.label: A
children.text: to develop and deploy advanced solutions to prevent, prepare, and protect against unintentional and intentional threats to agriculture and food in the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: to overcome barriers in the development of agricultural technologies, research tools, and qualified products and projects that enhance export competitiveness, environmental sustainability, and resilience to extreme weather;

children.label: C
children.text: to ensure that the United States maintains and enhances its position as a leader in developing and deploying agricultural technologies, research tools, and qualified projects and products that increase economic opportunities and security for farmers, ranchers, and rural communities; and

children.label: D
children.text: to undertake advanced research and development in areas in which industry by itself is not likely to do so because of the technological or financial uncertainty.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The AGARDA shall be a component of the Office of the Chief Scientist.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The AGARDA shall be headed by a Director, who shall be appointed by the Chief Scientist.

children.label: I
children.text: qualified products and projects;

children.label: II
children.text: agricultural technologies;

children.label: III
children.text: research tools; and

children.label: IV
children.text: challenges relating to the matters described in subclauses (I) through (III).

children.heading: Relationship within the Department of Agriculture
children.label: iii Relationship within the Department of Agriculture
children.text: The Director shall report to the Chief Scientist.

children.label: A
children.text: identifying and promoting advances in basic sciences;

children.label: B
children.text: translating scientific discoveries and inventions into technological innovations;

children.label: C
children.text: collaborating with other agencies, relevant industries, academia, international agencies, the Foundation for Food and Agriculture Research, and other relevant persons to carry out the goals described in paragraph (2), including convening, at a minimum, annual meetings or working groups to demonstrate the operation and effectiveness of advanced research and development of qualified products and projects, agricultural technologies, and research tools;

children.label: D
children.text: conducting ongoing searches for, and support calls for, potential advanced research and development of agricultural technologies, qualified products and projects, and research tools;

children.label: E
children.text: awarding grants and entering into contracts, cooperative agreements, or other transactions under paragraph (6) for advanced research and development of agricultural technology, qualified products and projects, and research tools;

children.label: i
children.text: are composed of representatives from Federal and State agencies, professional groups, academia, and industry;

children.label: ii
children.text: seek novel and effective solutions; and

children.label: iii
children.text: encourage data sharing and translation of research to field use; and

children.label: G
children.text: serving as a resource for interested persons regarding requirements under relevant laws that impact the development, commercialization, and technology transfer of qualified products and projects, agricultural technologies, and research tools.

children.label: A
children.text: address critical research and development needs for technology for specialty crops; or

children.label: B
children.text: prevent, protect, and prepare against intentional and unintentional threats to agriculture and food.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In carrying out the pilot program under this section, the Secretary shall have the authority to enter into other transactions in the same manner and subject to the same terms and conditions as transactions that the Secretary of Defense may enter into under .section 4021 of title 10

children.heading: Scope
children.label: B Scope
children.text: The authority of the Secretary to enter into contracts, cooperative agreements, and other transactions under this subsection shall be in addition to the authorities under this chapter and title I of the Department of Agriculture and Related Agencies Appropriation Act, 1964 (), to use contracts, cooperative agreements, and grants in carrying out the pilot program under this section.7 U.S.C. 3318a

children.heading: Guidelines
children.label: C Guidelines
children.text: The Secretary shall establish guidelines regarding the use of the authority under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Technology transfer
children.label: D Technology transfer
children.text: In entering into other transactions, the Secretary may negotiate terms for technology transfer in the same manner as a Federal laboratory under paragraphs (1) through (4) of .section 3710a(b) of title 15

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall require that, as a condition of being awarded a contract or grant or entering into a cooperative agreement or other transaction under paragraph (4)(E), a person shall make available to the Secretary on an ongoing basis, and submit to the Secretary on request of the Secretary, all data relating to or resulting from the activities carried out by the person pursuant to this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: This subparagraph shall be considered a statute described in .section 552(b)(3)(B) of title 5

children.label: I
children.text: Specific technical data or scientific information that is created or obtained under this section that reveals significant and not otherwise publicly known vulnerabilities of existing agriculture and food defenses against biological, chemical, nuclear, or radiological threats.

children.label: II
children.text: Trade secrets or commercial or financial information that is privileged or confidential (within the meaning of ) and obtained in the conduct of research or as a result of activities under this section from a non-Federal party participating in a contract, grant, cooperative agreement, or other transaction under this section.section 552(b)(4) of title 5

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: iii Limitation
children.text: Information that results from research and development activities conducted under this section and that would be a trade secret or commercial or financial information that is privileged or confidential if the information had been obtained from a non-Federal party participating in a cooperative agreement or other transaction shall be withheld from disclosure under subchapter II of chapter 5 of title 5 for 5 years.

children.label: A
children.text: use milestone-based awards and payments; and

children.label: B
children.text: terminate a project for not meeting technical milestones.

children.heading: Use of existing personnel authorities
children.label: 9 Use of existing personnel authorities
children.text: In carrying out this subsection, the Secretary may appoint highly qualified individuals to scientific or professional positions on the same terms and conditions as provided in subsections (b)(3), (b)(4), (c), (d), (e), and (f) of .section 7657 of this title

children.heading: Report
children.label: A Report
children.text: The Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate an annual report examining the actions undertaken and results generated by the AGARDA.

children.label: i
children.text: to be completed and submitted to the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate not later than 1 year after the date on which the Comptroller General began conducting the evaluation;

children.label: ii
children.text: describing the extent to which the AGARDA is achieving the goals described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: iii
children.text: including a recommendation on whether the AGARDA should be continued, terminated, or expanded.

children.label: A
children.text: to make determinations for future investments during the period of effectiveness of this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: to achieve the goals described in subsection (b)(2).

children.heading: Dissemination
children.label: 2 Dissemination
children.text: The Secretary shall disseminate the information contained in the strategic plan under paragraph (1) to persons who may have the capacity to substantially contribute to the activities described in that strategic plan.

children.label: A
children.text: update and coordinate the strategic coordination plan under with the strategic plan developed under paragraph (1) for activities relating to agriculture and food defense countermeasure development and procurement; andsection 6922(d)(7) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the National Agricultural Research, Extension, Education, and Economics Advisory Board established under ;section 3123(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the specialty crops committee established under ;section 3123a(a)(1) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: relevant agriculture research agencies of the Federal Government;

children.label: iv
children.text: the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine;

children.label: v
children.text: the National Veterinary Stockpile Intra-Government Advisory Committee for Strategic Steering; and

children.label: vi
children.text: other appropriate parties, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: for the purpose of carrying out this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the same manner and subject to the same terms and conditions as are applicable to the Secretary of Defense under .section 4021 of title 10

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Director, may accept and deposit into the Fund monies received pursuant to cost recovery, contribution, or royalty payments under a contract, grant, cooperative agreement, or other transaction under this section.

children.heading: Availability of amounts in fund
children.label: B Availability of amounts in fund
children.text: Amounts deposited into the fund shall remain available until expended, without further appropriation, and may be used to carry out the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: C Clarification
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph authorizes the use of the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation to carry out this section.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 3 Funding
children.text: In addition to funds otherwise deposited in the Fund under paragraph (1) or (2), there is authorized to be appropriated to the Fund $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided under paragraph (2), the authority provided by this section terminates on the date that is 5 years after .December 20, 2018

children.label: A
children.text: subsection (b)(7)(B); and

children.label: B
children.text: grants awarded or contracts, cooperative agreements, or other transactions entered into before the end of the 5-year period referred to in such clause.1So in original. Probably should be “such paragraph.”.1
